Apple___Apple_scab.symptoms: Olive-green to brown velvety spots on leaves, dark scabby lesions on fruit surface, premature leaf and fruit drop.
Apple___Apple_scab.treatment: Apply fungicide (myclobutanil or captan) at bud break. Remove and destroy infected leaves and fruit. Prune to improve air circulation.
Apple___Apple_scab.prevention: Plant scab-resistant apple varieties. Rake and destroy fallen leaves in autumn. Apply dormant lime sulfur spray before bud break.

Apple___Black_rot.symptoms: Circular brown lesions with purple borders on leaves, black rotting fruit with concentric rings, cankers on branches.
Apple___Black_rot.treatment: Remove and destroy all infected fruit, leaves and mummified fruit. Apply captan or thiophanate-methyl fungicide. Prune dead or cankered wood.
Apple___Black_rot.prevention: Maintain tree vigor with proper fertilization. Avoid wounding trees. Remove wild or abandoned apple trees nearby.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surface in spring, tube-like structures on undersides of leaves, deformed fruit.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.treatment: Apply myclobutanil or mancozeb fungicide starting at pink bud stage. Repeat every 7-10 days during wet weather.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.prevention: Remove nearby eastern red cedar or juniper trees. Plant rust-resistant apple varieties like Liberty or Freedom.

Apple___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Leaves appear green and vigorous.
Apple___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Your apple plant is healthy!
Apple___healthy.prevention: Continue regular pruning, balanced fertilization, and monitoring for early signs of pests or disease.

Blueberry___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Plant appears healthy and vigorous.
Blueberry___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Your blueberry plant is healthy!
Blueberry___healthy.prevention: Maintain acidic soil pH (4.5-5.5), adequate irrigation, and annual pruning for best results.

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery fungal growth on young leaves and shoots, leaf curling and distortion, reduced fruit size and quality.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.treatment: Apply sulfur-based or potassium bicarbonate fungicide. Remove and destroy infected shoots. Improve air circulation by pruning.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization. Plant in full sun with good air circulation. Use resistant cherry varieties where available.

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Cherry plant looks healthy.
Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Your cherry plant is healthy!
Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.prevention: Regular pruning to maintain open canopy, balanced watering, and annual inspection for pests and disease.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.symptoms: Rectangular tan to gray lesions running parallel to leaf veins, lesions can merge causing large areas of dead leaf tissue, reduced photosynthesis.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.treatment: Apply strobilurin or triazole fungicides at early disease onset. Scout fields regularly and apply at VT/R1 growth stage if disease pressure is high.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids. Rotate crops with non-host crops like soybean. Till crop residue to reduce pathogen survival.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.symptoms: Small oval to elongated brick-red to brown pustules scattered across both leaf surfaces, pustules rupture and release powdery rust-colored spores.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.treatment: Apply fungicides containing propiconazole or azoxystrobin if infection is severe before tasseling. Economic threshold is typically 50%+ leaf area infected before tasseling.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.prevention: Plant rust-resistant corn hybrids. Early planting can help avoid peak rust season. Monitor fields regularly during the growing season.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.symptoms: Large cigar-shaped gray-green to tan lesions 2.5-15 cm long on leaves, lesions may have wavy margins, severe infection causes premature plant death.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.treatment: Apply fungicides (azoxystrobin, propiconazole) at early disease onset, especially if upper leaves are infected before or during silking.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids. Rotate corn with soybean or other non-host crops. Manage crop residues by tillage.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Corn plant appears healthy.
Corn_(maize)___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Your corn plant is healthy!
Corn_(maize)___healthy.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds, maintain proper plant spacing, and practice crop rotation annually.

Grape___Black_rot.symptoms: Small brown circular spots with dark borders on leaves, infected berries turn brown then black and shrivel into hard mummified fruit.
Grape___Black_rot.treatment: Apply mancozeb or myclobutanil fungicide from bud break through veraison. Remove mummified berries and infected leaves promptly.
Grape___Black_rot.prevention: Remove mummified fruit before new growth starts. Prune for good air circulation. Apply dormant fungicide sprays.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).symptoms: Tiger-stripe pattern of yellow/red discoloration on leaves, dark brown streaks inside wood, berries develop dark spots and may shrivel, sudden vine collapse in severe cases.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).treatment: No curative treatment available. Remove and destroy severely infected vines. Protect pruning wounds with fungicide or wound sealant immediately after pruning.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).prevention: Avoid large pruning wounds. Disinfect pruning tools between vines. Apply trichoderma-based biocontrol agents to pruning wounds.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.symptoms: Irregular dark brown to black spots on older leaves, spots may have yellow halos, severe infections cause premature defoliation reducing fruit quality.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.treatment: Apply copper-based fungicides or mancozeb. Remove infected leaves and destroy them. Ensure good canopy air circulation.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.prevention: Prune to open canopy for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Remove fallen infected leaves from the vineyard floor.

Grape___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Grapevine appears healthy.
Grape___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Your grapevine is healthy!
Grape___healthy.prevention: Annual pruning, balanced fertilization, and regular scouting for pests and disease keeps vines productive.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).symptoms: Asymmetric yellowing (blotchy mottle) of leaves, small lopsided bitter fruit, premature fruit drop, twig dieback, overall tree decline.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).treatment: No cure exists. Remove and destroy infected trees immediately to prevent spread. Control Asian citrus psyllid vector with systemic insecticides (imidacloprid).
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).prevention: Use certified disease-free nursery stock. Control psyllid populations aggressively. Quarantine new plant material. Report suspected infections to agriculture authorities.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small water-soaked spots on leaves turning brown with yellow halos, shot-hole appearance as spots fall out, sunken dark spots on fruit, twig cankers.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericides starting at bud swell. Repeat applications every 7-10 days during wet weather. Avoid excessive nitrogen.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Plant resistant peach varieties. Windbreaks reduce spread. Avoid overhead irrigation. Prune to improve air circulation.

Peach___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Peach plant appears healthy.
Peach___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Your peach plant is healthy!
Peach___healthy.prevention: Annual dormant sprays, proper thinning, and regular inspection for pests maintain peach tree health.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small water-soaked circular spots on leaves turning brown with yellow halos, raised scabby spots on fruit, severe defoliation in humid conditions.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper hydroxide or copper sulfate bactericide. Remove infected plant debris. Avoid working in fields when plants are wet.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds. Treat seeds with hot water (50°C for 25 minutes). Practice 2-3 year crop rotation.

Pepper,_bell___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Bell pepper plant appears healthy.
Pepper,_bell___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Your bell pepper plant is healthy!
Pepper,_bell___healthy.prevention: Use disease-free transplants, practice crop rotation, and maintain consistent soil moisture for healthy plants.

Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown to black circular spots with concentric rings (target-board pattern) on older leaves, yellow halo around spots, premature leaf drop.
Potato___Early_blight.treatment: Apply chlorothalonil, mancozeb, or azoxystrobin fungicide at first sign of disease. Repeat every 7-14 days. Remove severely infected leaves.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Use certified disease-free seed potatoes. Rotate crops. Maintain adequate plant nutrition especially potassium. Avoid overhead irrigation.

Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Water-soaked pale green to brown lesions on leaves with white fuzzy growth on undersides in humid conditions, rapid collapse of foliage, dark rotting tubers.
Potato___Late_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides (metalaxyl, cymoxanil, mancozeb) immediately at first sign. Destroy infected plant material. Do not harvest tubers for 2 weeks after killing tops.
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Use certified disease-free seed potatoes. Plant resistant varieties. Apply preventive fungicide sprays during wet weather. Destroy volunteer potato plants.

Potato___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Potato plant appears healthy.
Potato___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Your potato plant is healthy!
Potato___healthy.prevention: Use certified seed potatoes, practice 3-4 year crop rotation, and hill plants properly to protect developing tubers.

Raspberry___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Raspberry plant appears healthy.
Raspberry___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Your raspberry plant is healthy!
Raspberry___healthy.prevention: Prune out old canes after fruiting, maintain good air circulation, and avoid overhead irrigation to prevent disease.

Soybean___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Soybean plant appears healthy.
Soybean___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Your soybean plant is healthy!
Soybean___healthy.prevention: Use certified seeds, practice crop rotation with corn, and scout fields regularly during the growing season.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery fungal coating on upper leaf surfaces, leaves turn yellow and brown, premature leaf death reduces fruit yield and quality.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.treatment: Apply potassium bicarbonate, sulfur, or neem oil spray at first sign. Kaolin clay sprays can also help. Remove severely infected leaves.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Space plants for good air circulation. Avoid overhead watering. Apply preventive neem oil sprays.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.symptoms: Small irregular dark purple spots on upper leaf surface, spots enlarge and centers turn tan to brown giving a scorched appearance, leaf margins turn purple to brown.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.treatment: Apply captan or myclobutanil fungicide. Remove infected leaves. Avoid overhead irrigation and ensure good drainage.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.prevention: Use certified disease-free transplants. Renovate plantings annually. Practice 3-year crop rotation. Remove old infected leaves.

Strawberry___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Strawberry plant appears healthy.
Strawberry___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Your strawberry plant is healthy!
Strawberry___healthy.prevention: Use certified transplants, renovate beds annually, and maintain weed-free rows for best strawberry health.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small water-soaked circular spots on leaves turning dark brown with yellow halos, raised scabby spots on fruit, severe defoliation exposing fruit to sunscald.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper bactericide plus mancozeb. Remove infected plant parts. Avoid overhead irrigation. Do not work with plants when wet.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use disease-free certified seeds. Hot water seed treatment (50°C for 25 min). Rotate with non-solanaceous crops for 2-3 years.

Tomato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown to black circular lesions with concentric rings (target-board pattern) on older lower leaves, yellow halo around lesions, premature defoliation.
Tomato___Early_blight.treatment: Apply chlorothalonil, mancozeb, or copper fungicide at first sign. Repeat every 7-10 days. Remove and destroy infected lower leaves.
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or transplants. Stake plants for air circulation. Avoid overhead watering. Mulch to reduce soil splash.

Tomato___Late_blight.symptoms: Large irregular water-soaked pale green lesions on leaves, white mold on undersides in humid weather, dark brown greasy spots on stems, brown rotting fruit.
Tomato___Late_blight.treatment: Apply copper-based or chlorothalonil fungicide immediately. Remove and bag infected plants. Do not compost infected material. Apply preventively during cool wet weather.
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Use resistant tomato varieties. Avoid overhead irrigation. Improve air circulation. Destroy infected debris. Apply preventive fungicide during cool wet periods.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Pale greenish-yellow spots on upper leaf surface, olive-green to brown velvety mold growth on lower leaf surface, leaves curl upward and eventually die.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.treatment: Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil or copper. Reduce humidity in greenhouses. Improve ventilation. Remove infected leaves.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention: Maintain humidity below 85%. Ensure good greenhouse ventilation. Space plants adequately. Use resistant tomato varieties. Avoid wetting leaves.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms: Many small circular spots with dark brown borders and gray-white centers on lower leaves, tiny black dots visible in spot centers, severe defoliation from bottom up.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.treatment: Apply chlorothalonil, copper, or mancozeb fungicide at first sign. Remove infected leaves. Avoid overhead watering. Stake plants.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention: Use disease-free seeds. Rotate tomatoes with non-solanaceous crops for 2 years. Mulch heavily. Stake or cage plants for air circulation.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.symptoms: Tiny yellow or white speckles on upper leaf surface (stippling), fine webbing on undersides of leaves, leaves turn bronze then yellow and drop, plant stunting in severe cases.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.treatment: Apply miticides (abamectin, bifenazate) or insecticidal soap. Spray forcefully with water to dislodge mites. Introduce predatory mites (Phytoseiulus persimilis) for biological control.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.prevention: Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization. Maintain adequate irrigation. Conserve natural predators. Scout plants regularly especially in hot dry conditions.

Tomato___Target_Spot.symptoms: Circular brown lesions with concentric rings and yellow halos on leaves, dark sunken spots on fruit, premature defoliation starting from lower leaves.
Tomato___Target_Spot.treatment: Apply azoxystrobin or chlorothalonil fungicide at disease onset. Remove infected lower leaves. Improve air circulation by staking and pruning.
Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Stake and prune plants for good air flow. Avoid overhead irrigation. Rotate crops annually.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptoms: Upward curling and yellowing of young leaves, leaf edges turn yellow, plant stunting, flower drop, drastically reduced fruit set and yield.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.treatment: No cure once infected. Remove and destroy infected plants immediately to prevent spread. Control whitefly vectors with imidacloprid or thiamethoxam insecticides.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention: Use TYLCV-resistant tomato varieties. Control whitefly populations with reflective mulches and yellow sticky traps. Use insect-proof netting in seedling nurseries.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Light and dark green mosaic mottling pattern on leaves, leaf distortion and puckering, fern-leaf symptom on young growth, stunted plants, internal browning of fruit.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.treatment: No cure available. Remove and destroy infected plants. Disinfect tools with 10% bleach solution. Wash hands thoroughly after handling infected plants.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: Use certified virus-free seeds or resistant varieties. Control aphid vectors. Disinfect tools regularly. Do not smoke near plants (tobacco can carry virus).

Tomato___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Tomato plant appears healthy.
Tomato___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Your tomato plant is healthy!
Tomato___healthy.prevention: Use disease-free transplants, practice crop rotation, stake plants for air circulation, and water at the base to keep foliage dry.
